entries.statement: Knowledge of enterprise data strategy frameworks, including data governance operating models, data architecture principles, and the organizational interdependencies that determine data program effectiveness.
Knowledge of enterprise risk management frameworks and how data risk, AI risk, and technology risk are integrated into enterprise risk reporting and board-level risk governance.

Knowledge of data governance program design, including operating model options such as federated, centralized, and hub-and-spoke structures, council structures, policy frameworks, stewardship network models, escalation path architectures, and the organizational conditions under which each model is appropriate.

Knowledge of data catalog and metadata management platforms and standards, including active metadata principles, automated lineage tracking, business glossary governance and conventions, and data catalog tooling used to document and publish data asset definitions.
Knowledge of data maturity assessment frameworks, including DCAM, DAMA DMBOK, and CMMI-DMM, and how maturity scores translate to organizational investment priorities.
Knowledge of AI model lifecycle management, including model versioning, drift detection, performance monitoring, retraining triggers, and decommissioning criteria.
Knowledge of data policy authoring, including policy structure standards, enforcement mechanisms, exception handling processes, and policy lifecycle management.
Knowledge of data domain and data ownership concepts, including how to define domain boundaries, assign accountability, and resolve cross-domain ownership disputes.
Knowledge of the data domain for which stewardship responsibility is held, including its business purpose, system sources, consumer use cases, and applicable regulatory classifications.
